title: regional-capacity-abacus-spot-reserve-plan
preview:
Purpose:
Quick exploratory sheet to bucketize capacity by region and purchase type (spot vs reserved), estimate GPU-hour burn for three demand profiles, and produce an approximate $/1M-token metric that factors in utilization, batching, and quantization mixes. This is an operational-first working draft — assumptions...
text:
Purpose:
Quick exploratory sheet to bucketize capacity by region and purchase type (spot vs reserved), estimate GPU-hour burn for three demand profiles, and produce an approximate $/1M-token metric that factors in utilization, batching, and quantization mixes. This is an operational-first working draft — assumptions below need validation with procurement and finance.

Summary takeaways (top-line):
- Baseline steady-state: 120 A100-80GB equiv GPU-hours/day across regions -> ~3.6M tokens/day at medium prompt length (avg 128 tokens) assuming 10k tokens/GPU-hour after batching/kv-cache. Estimated cost ~ $18 / 1M tokens in US regions if using a 60% reserved / 40% spot mix.
- High-demand spike (2x baseline): token unit cost increases ~25% due to spot unavailability and short-term burst to on-demand H100 equivalents.
- Aggressive quantization (int4) + improved batching -> tokens/GPU-hour inc...

title: Contrast eval: rerank slices & NDCG notes
preview:
Purpose and context:
This note captures a compact protocol and pragmatic heuristics we used during the latest model onboarding (Feb 2026) for reranking. Goal: produce an evaluation manifest that is cheap to label with pairwise preferences, supports robust NDCG calculations, and surfaces per-slice regressions we can ...
text:
Purpose and context:
This note captures a compact protocol and pragmatic heuristics we used during the latest model onboarding (Feb 2026) for reranking. Goal: produce an evaluation manifest that is cheap to label with pairwise preferences, supports robust NDCG calculations, and surfaces per-slice regressions we can act on quickly. This is intentionally pragmatic — aim for high signal / low labeling cost.

High-level approach (one-paragraph):
- Sample diverse query buckets (query-intent, traffic-volume, tail-phrases). For each query, create a candidate pool from the production candidate set + model variants. Convert into pairwise comparisons by sampling anchor-candidate pairs biased toward position changes (i.e., where new model would move an item relative to baseline). Use binary preference labels with forced ties allowed (0=tie, 1=prefer-A, 2=prefer-B). Compute NDCG at k with mean ov...

title: Retrieval backpressure & prefetch failure modes (8k/32k/128k)
preview:
SUMMARY:
This working note captures exploratory evals and observed failure modes for retrieval-heavy long-context scenarios across 8k / 32k / 128k effective context lengths. The focus is on two operational stress behaviors we saw in onboarding models: (1) backpressure from the retrieval subsystem when downstream tok...
text:
SUMMARY:
This working note captures exploratory evals and observed failure modes for retrieval-heavy long-context scenarios across 8k / 32k / 128k effective context lengths. The focus is on two operational stress behaviors we saw in onboarding models: (1) backpressure from the retrieval subsystem when downstream tokenization/KV-cache growth outpaces serving throughput, and (2) prefetch + concatenation failure modes that lead to truncation, attention-sink artifacts, or degraded output quality.

Purpose and scope:
- Produce a compact matrix of test cases (prefetch enabled/disabled, chunking strategies, sentinel-token injection) for regression harnesses.
- Surface quantitative and qualitative signals to inform model onboarding acceptance criteria.
- Propose mitigations that are practical to implement in the serving runtime and the retrieval layer.

TEST MATRIX (high level):
- Context siz...

text:
Purpose:
This is a working sprint-notes doc for FlashLab: a focused 2-day mini-sprint to validate SDK onboarding behavior under production-like incident patterns. Goal is a lightweight, repeatable kit teams can run in the Playground to reproduce common failure modes, capture telemetry, and confirm SDK fallbacks and user-facing messaging.

Audience:
- Developer Experience engineers (owners)
- Solutions and SRE on-call engineers (observers)
- Product PMs triaging SDK UX regressions

Sprint hypothesis:
If we provide a 15-minute incident-sim harness that toggles network flakiness, model variance fallbacks, and prefix cache evictions, engineers will reproduce 80% of reported onboarding issues and iterate fixes within a single remediation cycle.

Success criteria (minimum):
- A runnable script that 3 engineers can execute in < 15m to simulate: transient 503s, 100–300ms jitter, and model-var...

title: Mode-specific networking strata and tenant circuits
preview:
Purpose
This note collects pragmatic, mode-specific networking and tenant-circuit guidance for Dedicated, Hosted, and Private deployments. Intended audience: platform engineers designing routing policy, SREs who operate multi-region control planes, and solutions engineers who map customer requirements to deployment ...
text:
Purpose
This note collects pragmatic, mode-specific networking and tenant-circuit guidance for Dedicated, Hosted, and Private deployments. Intended audience: platform engineers designing routing policy, SREs who operate multi-region control planes, and solutions engineers who map customer requirements to deployment modes.

Scope and constraints
- Focus: control-plane vs data-plane routing boundaries, tenant isolation mechanisms, and multi-region failover patterns.
- Not covered in depth: VM-level OS hardening, customer-side network topology beyond egress rules, or specific cloud provider CLI commands.
- Assumption: Serving runtime can be deployed as containerized GPU pools and supports label-based model variants.

High-level mental model
- Control plane: orchestration, model catalogs, rollout state, policy decisions (who can route where). Should be multi-region-aware but not necessari...

title: Module telemetry and signal contracts (draft)
preview: Context: We maintain many terraform primitives (vpc, iam, eks/gke/aks) that are consumed by onprem/vpc customers and internal cluster factories. Historically the upgrade pain has come from unclear output semantics, missing runtime signals (how to know whether a module altered networking in-place vs replaced), and di...
text:
Context: We maintain many terraform primitives (vpc, iam, eks/gke/aks) that are consumed by onprem/vpc customers and internal cluster factories. Historically the upgrade pain has come from unclear output semantics, missing runtime signals (how to know whether a module altered networking in-place vs replaced), and differing expectations across cloud providers. This doc sketches a telemetry-and-signal-first design to reduce ambiguity during upgrades and to make automated validation possible.

Goals:
- Make module changes observable: every release should expose a consistent set ofSignals/metrics so automation and SRE can detect semantic changes quickly.
- Keep upgrade risk low: provide clear contract on outputs and variables, plus a minimal canary/rollback plan for infra teams and customers.
- Enable automated gating in CI and ClusterFactory pipelines by asserting signals and outputs bef...

title: Control Plane Delegation Patterns for Hosted / Dedicated / Private
preview:
Purpose:

Capture pragmatic control-plane delegation patterns and operational tradeoffs for Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployment modes. This note focuses on how control-plane responsibilities (routing decisions, tenant metadata, failover policies) should be delegated or centralized to balance isolation, SLOs, a...
text:
Purpose:

Capture pragmatic control-plane delegation patterns and operational tradeoffs for Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployment modes. This note focuses on how control-plane responsibilities (routing decisions, tenant metadata, failover policies) should be delegated or centralized to balance isolation, SLOs, and operational cost.

Executive summary (short):
- There is no single right model. Use a small, documented set of delegation patterns and map them to customer requirements (isolation, sovereignty, latency, ops autonomy).
- Patterns: Centralized master control-plane, Regional delegates, Tenant-local control-plane agents (sidecars), and Hybrid delegated-control (control plane with leased-per-tenant slices).
- For Hosted: default to centralized control-plane with regional delegates for latency and scale; provide isolation fences via routing policies and per-tenant cryptographi...

title: Action invocation testplan - sandbox notes (WIP)
preview:

This is a lightweight working-plan for a sandboxed evaluation harness focused on action/invocation behavior for model onboarding. Goal is to surface class-level regressions across model variants (float16/quantized/int8-friendly), prompt styles, and multi-step flows before we promote a model into the nightly...
text:
Purpose:
This is a lightweight working-plan for a sandboxed evaluation harness focused on action/invocation behavior for model onboarding. Goal is to surface class-level regressions across model variants (float16/quantized/int8-friendly), prompt styles, and multi-step flows before we promote a model into the nightly regression suite or Dedicated rollout.

Scope:
- Evaluate parsing of structured arguments returned by the model (JSON/kwargs-like text) for a representative set of tools (search, calculator, calendar, http-fetch).
- Measure selection accuracy: does the model select the correct tool given ambiguous and partial intents.
- Validate multi-step flows: stateful handoffs where a tool result is consumed by the model and a subsequent action is chosen.
- Identify flaky edge cases that differ across kernels/quantization and prompt templates.

Why sandbox?
- Fast iteration with local ...

preview:

text:
Purpose:
This is a working rehearsal playbook for Kubernetes node pool GPU driver and runtime upgrades in on-prem/VPC-private clusters. Focus is on validating compat matrices (k8s / kernel / NVIDIA driver / CUDA / NCCL / container runtime) and capturing Terraform module knobs and safe rollouts. This is intentionally informal — notes, sample tfvars, known failure modes, and manual checks to run before/after.

Scope and assumptions:
- Target clusters: on-prem vSphere-based clusters and bare-metal racks running kubeadm or Rancher-managed k8s.
- Node GPUs: mixed NVIDIA A10 / A100 / L4. Some nodes have MIG enabled.
- Container runtime: containerd 1.7.x (some older nodes using containerd 1.6); Docker is deprecated but one legacy node still runs dockerd.
- TF modules: module private-deploy/gke_like/vsphere-nodepool and module driver-installer (provisioner-based).

High-level validation matri...

title: Backcasted regional GPU surge timeline and utilization sensitivity
preview:

This worksheet contains a backcast-informed forecast and rollout timeline for GPU capacity across NA / EU / APAC for the 12-month horizon starting 2026-03. The model uses trailing three-month usage patterns, observed latency SLO violations, sales demand signals, and known vendor onboarding window...
text:
Executive summary:

This worksheet contains a backcast-informed forecast and rollout timeline for GPU capacity across NA / EU / APAC for the 12-month horizon starting 2026-03. The model uses trailing three-month usage patterns, observed latency SLO violations, sales demand signals, and known vendor onboarding windows. It focuses on three deliverables: (1) a phased capacity insertion timeline, (2) utilization sensitivity scenarios (base / +30% demand / -20% demand), and (3) operational triggers for burst/eviction and fallback routing.

Key assumptions and inputs (short):
- Baseline token throughput per GPU is derived from production K80-equivalent normalized vAUs measured Nov 2025 (internal benchmark INF-BENCH-11).
- Average effective throughput per slot: NA: 3.6M tokens/day, EU: 3.2M, APAC: 2.9M (includes regional burst inefficiency 5%).
- Planned vendor deliveries: Q2 2026 NA 120 GPU...

text:
Purpose:
- Practical working playbook for stitching small synthetic probes together into a dashboard spotlight view that is cheap to run and useful for fast oncall triage.
- Emphasis: actionable signal, low query cost, fast triage path from alert -> trace anchor -> snapshot panel.

Scope and constraints:
- Target: multi-tenant inference runtime (text generation + embeddings).
- Keep probe cost < $0.10/day per 100 tenants (budgeted).
- Avoid heavy full-trace capture unless severity >= P1.
- Synthetic probes run from three regions (us-west, eu-west, ap-south) on staggered cadence.

Key concepts (operator language):
- Spotlight panel: small dashboard column composed of 6 micro-widgets that together answer "is system healthy for typical short chat requests?"
- Microprobe: a single synthetic request pattern (prompt, user/session headers, tenant-id) designed to exercise a particular path e....

Focus: inference-serving signals that matter for latency, cost, and output quality (including human-review paths). Goal: reduce dashb...
text:
Purpose and scope:
This doc is a pragmatic, incremental checklist for moving observability signals (metrics + traces + panels) from ad-hoc widgets to an automated dashboard lifecycle. Focus: inference-serving signals that matter for latency, cost, and output quality (including human-review paths). Goal: reduce dashboard churn, codify deprecation rules, and enable safe automation that can generate/update panels with confidence.

Why now:
- Panel sprawl is increasing and query cost is up 23% YOY across observability dashboards.
- Oncall friction when investigating quality regressions: operators can’t tell if a signal is stable or experimental.
- We have a set of repeatable generation scripts (obs-scripts) but lack maturity gating for signals.

Audience:
- Observability engineers, SREs, Feature owners (serving-runtime, hosted-api, dedicated), Dashboard curators.

Purpose and scope:

Themes cluster into three lanes: (A) token & latency surprises at cold starts and within multi-tenant Dedicated pools, (B) developer-facing error clarity for streaming and reconnects, (C) cost/quality tradeoff requests (recommendations for quantized model variants and auto-fallback). title: Translation quickcheck smoke corpus for CJK & RTL
preview:

This working doc collects a compact, high-signal "quickcheck" smoke corpus intended for model onboarding and fast regression checks focused on CJK (Chinese/Japanese/Korean), RTL (Arabic/Hebrew), and locale-specific formatting/translation issues. The goal is not exhaustive coverage but to provide...
text:
Purpose and context:
This working doc collects a compact, high-signal "quickcheck" smoke corpus intended for model onboarding and fast regression checks focused on CJK (Chinese/Japanese/Korean), RTL (Arabic/Hebrew), and locale-specific formatting/translation issues. The goal is not exhaustive coverage but to provide bite-sized, repeatable tests that catch common classes of failures quickly during model swaps, quantization changes, or tokenizer/kernel updates.

How to use this doc:
- During initial onboarding: run the full onboarding eval but also include these quickcheck prompts as a sanity gate (must-pass criteria).
- During CI: run these prompts as part of a nightly smoke job against the candidate serving image/quantized artifact.
- For regressions: add failing prompts to the dataset with a short note of root cause and expected behavior.

text:
Purpose:
This doc is a compact, editable checklist + blocker registry used during inference rollouts. Designed for Customer Success (CS) owners to run weekly syncs with customers and internal teams, capture ownership, track active blockers, and surface risk to renewals. Use as the single source of truth in customer status updates and QBR prep.

How to use (quick):
- Update the milestone rows during weekly CS standups.
- Mark blocker severity and assign a mitigation owner (internal/external).
- Attach or link tickets in 'linked_artifacts' or add a Jira key inline.
- Escalate to Solutions Eng or SRE when severity=high and ETA slips >3 days.

Fields explained (row items you should maintain):
- Milestone: short name for the deliverable (e.g., "Baseline model validation").
- Owner: person responsible (CS, SE, or customer PO).
- Target date: committed date for milestone completion.
- Status...

title: Truncation edgecases and prompt anchoring hypothesis
preview:
Purpose:
This is a working note for a focused set of long-context stress tests that probe truncation boundary behaviors and an experimental "prompt anchoring" technique intended to reduce information loss when the model must attend to earlier retrieved context under 8k/32k/128k regimes. Primary goal is to identify r...
text:
Purpose:
This is a working note for a focused set of long-context stress tests that probe truncation boundary behaviors and an experimental "prompt anchoring" technique intended to reduce information loss when the model must attend to earlier retrieved context under 8k/32k/128k regimes. Primary goal is to identify robust signal(s) for regressions when onboarding new model variants and quantization profiles.

Background / motivation:
- We've seen flaky recovery when retrieved documents are appended near the left of the prompt and the model is forced to truncate; downstream hallucination or missing facts spikes in some models/quantization combos.
- Existing long-context harnesses measure token-level recall and end-to-end QA F1, but they don't isolate whether failure is due to attention sink (model focusing on dense trailing context) or hard truncation removing critical anchor tokens.
- ...

The goal is to validate operational assumptions, measure evidence size/provenance, and exercise customer-managed-ne...
text:
Purpose
---
This is a lightweight PRD-style playbook for a focused playtest: exercise approval gates and audit-log retention semantics in Private deployments using synthetic (fabricated) change events. The goal is to validate operational assumptions, measure evidence size/provenance, and exercise customer-managed-network handshakes without touching real customer configs.

Why this helps
---
- Low-risk way to verify that approval sampling, compact evidence bundles, and rollback tokens behave end-to-end in on-prem environments.
- Lets us measure network cost, archive size, and query latency for common forensic queries before committing to a retention/ingest model.
- Provides concrete UX/automation gaps for operator-side agents and control-plane attestation.

Scope (what we will test)
---

preview:

text:
Purpose:
This is a working checklist + scenario bank for onboarding retrieval-augmented models that will answer contract-related questions. Focus is on quote fidelity, grounding to source contract text, and safe refusal behavior for legal-advice/disallowed requests. Use this as the canonical quick-run for pre-launch regression and as a handoff to SRE/eval pipelines.

Quick notes / context:
- Target customers: platform teams embedding contract search/responses into contract review assistants and CLM UIs.
- Threat model: hallucinated clause numbers, invented obligations, partial/misleading quotes, mistaken cross-document attribution.
- Primary goal: avoid returning a misattributed or fabricated contractual obligation. Secondary: good UX (concise answers, clear citations).

How to use this doc (recommended flow):

Overview:
This playbook documents the applied-ML onboarding and regression strategy for Aurora Payments (merchant-facing transaction classification and dispute-assistant features). - Product surface evaluated: 1) transaction-labeling API (category, risk_flag, merchant_intent), 2) dispute-assistant conversational flows (clarifying questions + structured dispute JSON output).
- Risk profile: low tolerance for false negatives in fraud/risk flags; strict PII handling; latency sensitivity in stream route (p95 target).

preview:

It assumes conversion is driven by performance and business need rather than a simple ti...
text:
Purpose:
This playbook is a practical, low-friction plan for converting high-performing contractors into permanent employees with a focus on early integration, calibrated interview scoring, and manager-led retention touchpoints. It assumes conversion is driven by performance and business need rather than a simple time-based trigger.

Scope and audience:
- Audience: people-ops, hiring managers, recruiting partners, hiring panel members, and the conversions review committee.
- Scope: contractors in IC and engineering-adjacent roles (SWE, SRE, Applied ML infra, Product/PM), for mid-to-senior levels. Not intended for executive-level conversions (separate approval flow).

Why a different approach?
Contractor conversions are high-leverage hires: these candidates already understand product and culture. This playbook reduces duplication (don’t repeat full external hire loop), focuses intervie...

Purpose:
This doc captures a pragmatic acceptance-test checklist and telemetry spec for validating customer region-pinning commitments during onboarding and major changes. It is intentionally practical: minimal friction for SREs/SEs to run, and machine-checkable where possible.

Scope and goals:
- Validate that a given customer configuration routes data to allowed regions only (pin enforcement).
- Provide concrete test vectors for both happy-path and fallback/crossover scenarios (e.g., planned failovers, capacity constraints, user exceptions).
- Define the telemetry events, labels, and sampling needed to prove compliance to customers and auditors without leaking customer PII.
- Produce a checklist that can be executed manually or automated by the residency test harness (infra job).

text:
Purpose:
This is a pragmatic, portable field guide for operators doing a day-one install and the first scale/rotation cycles for the Private Control Plane (VPC / on-prem). It is intentionally <informal> — bite-sized checklists, quick commands, and verification steps you can copy into a shell or ticket.

Scope and assumptions:
- Customer is provisioning a Private Control Plane (PCP) cluster that will manage inference nodes.
- Terraform is the canonical provisioning mechanism; we expect operator to have the terraform repo checked out and AWS/GCP creds or on-prem provisioning access.
- Network team has reserved IP ranges, DNS delegation (subdomain for control-plane), and firewall rules ready.
- KMS/HSM integration is optional; steps below cover both software KMS and cloud KMS patterns.
- This doc focuses on initial bootstrap, adding capacity (1 -> N GPUs), KMS/key rotations, and rolling ...

title: Registry behavioral contracts and routing guidelines
preview:
Purpose

This is a working note describing the "behavioral contract" we expect between the model registry (control plane) and the routing/serving layer. Goal: make model version pinning predictable across regions while enabling safe rollouts and automated fallbacks.

Context and motivation

text:
Purpose

This is a working note describing the "behavioral contract" we expect between the model registry (control plane) and the routing/serving layer. Goal: make model version pinning predictable across regions while enabling safe rollouts and automated fallbacks.

Context and motivation
- Platform requirement: customers can pin routes to a model version (e.g., my-product@v2025-08-01) but routers must also respect service-level routing policies (latency SLOs, region affinity, cost tier).
- Current problem areas: ambiguous metadata in registry, inconsistent fallback behavior across regions, and hard-to-debug rollouts when registry updates are eventually consistent.

Key principles (high level)
1) Declarative, machine-readable contracts in the registry metadata. Runtime should not infer semantics from ad-hoc fields.
2) Fallbacks must be explicit and ordered; routers should not invent ...

Purpose:
This doc is a lean, pragmatic cookbook for the very first experience we want new SDK users to have. The emphasis is low friction, observable, and recoverable: get a successful end-to-end call (local or hosted) in under 5 minutes, show obvious next steps, and collect minimal telemetry so we can measure success.

Audience:
- Software engineers evaluating Redwood for product work
- New internal engineers trying the SDK for the first time
- Solutions engineers running customer demos

Design goals (quick reminders):
- Zero surprises: friendly errors, clear next steps, autofill examples
- Minimal cognitive load: one canonical path (npm/pip) + one sample app per language
- Local-first: enable iterating without creating cloud credentials, then a clear step to switch to hosted/de-dedicated/private
- Instrumented: opt-in telemetry hooks (latency, token usage, error class) documented an...

text:
Purpose:
This note is a practical playbook for running a gated, lane-based SDK onramp that uses pooled sandbox runners and lightweight chaos probes to validate SDK releases before a general rollout. Aim: reduce friction for new integrators while catching regressions in telemetry, latency-sensitive flows, and prompt-output regressions (prompt-linting step).

Audience:
- Developer Experience (owners)
- SDK maintainers
- Solutions engineers running customer pilots
- SRE/QA for chaos probe support

High-level approach (one-liner):
Gate every SDK release behind an automated onramp pipeline composed of 3 lanes (fast, steady, conservative) that exercise representative journeys using pooled ephemeral runners; inject controlled faults and record telemetry markers tied to token-level latency and output diffs.

Core components:
- Pool runners: small fleet of hermetic sandboxes (node/python conta...

text: Working operational plan to convert monthly regional forecasts into an actionable rolling buffer schedule across NA / EU / APAC. Focus is on fast reaction windows (weekly run rate checks), spot vs reserved mix, and thermal/instance-type constraints that affect effective capacity. This doc contains assumptions, 3 demand scenarios, an actionable trigger matrix, and a compact monthly heatmap (next 9 months) for planning and oncall playbooks.

title: SDK first-touch field scrapbook
preview:
Purpose
This is a lightweight, informal collection of things we observed during 1:1 onboarding sessions, early pilot installs, and guerrilla usability testing for the SDK quickstart. Aim: capture friction patterns, exact wording that confused users, and compact recipes we can fold into the official quickstart. Not a...
text:
Purpose
This is a lightweight, informal collection of things we observed during 1:1 onboarding sessions, early pilot installs, and guerrilla usability testing for the SDK quickstart. Aim: capture friction patterns, exact wording that confused users, and compact recipes we can fold into the official quickstart. Not a final playbook — a scrapbook of field-proven fixes and tiny experiments.

High-level takeaways (quick)
- First 2 minutes: most devs want one runnable command they can paste into a terminal. Anything that requires editing >1 file loses them.
- Error feedback matters more than config complexity: clear messages beat fewer options.
- Local network/proxy failure modes were common (corporate proxies, VPN). Include a hermetic fallback path.

Representative quotes (verbatim from sessions)
- "I just want to see something stream — tell me what to run in under 30s."
- "Why does it ne...

title: Lease & TTL Driven State Reconciliation and Routing Guidelines
preview:
Purpose:
This note captures a lightweight pattern for coupling short-lived leases/TTLs with anti-entropy reconciliation and routing policies to control staleness across regions. The goal is a pragmatic operating point: provide low-latency reads with bounded staleness and well-defined repair/rollback behavior under r...
text:
Purpose:
This note captures a lightweight pattern for coupling short-lived leases/TTLs with anti-entropy reconciliation and routing policies to control staleness across regions. The goal is a pragmatic operating point: provide low-latency reads with bounded staleness and well-defined repair/rollback behavior under region partitions, without requiring synchronous global consensus for every write.

Context and motivation:
- Our serving runtime needs very low tail latency for route decisions (model variant selection, cache hits, route-to-dedicated vs hosted) while also ensuring correctness for sensitive control-plane state (route policies, pinning, rollout markers).
- Fully strongly-consistent global state (single leader, paxos across regions) is costly in latency and fragile during partitions. A soft-state approach (leases + TTLs + background reconciliation) gives good availability and ...

title: Error budget forecasting & dashboard templates
preview:
Purpose:
This working doc proposes a lightweight forecasting approach for error budgets that ties SLO erosion to operational cost and trace signal quality. The goal is a set of metric definitions, a minimal trace sampling strategy for forecast inputs, and two dashboard templates (operational & executive) that surfac...
text:
Purpose:
This working doc proposes a lightweight forecasting approach for error budgets that ties SLO erosion to operational cost and trace signal quality. The goal is a set of metric definitions, a minimal trace sampling strategy for forecast inputs, and two dashboard templates (operational & executive) that surface predictive risk windows and remediation runbooks.

Context and motivation:
- We have strong SLI definitions for 99p latency and success-rate, but lack forward-looking visibility: teams discover budget burn only after incidents start.
- Forecasting SLO burn gives teams a runway to act (scale, rollback, reduce batch size, or enable fallback models) before customer impact.
- Forecasts must be cheap to compute and rely on surface telemetry already available in the runtime + a small set of targeted trace samples.

Audience / stakeholders:
- Primary: serving runtime oncall & pl...

text:
Purpose:
This sheet is a working simulator for APAC regional capacity under mixed quantization and batching profiles. Focus is on: expected throughput uplift, SLO tail-latency risk, and an estimate of end-user quality degradation (regression budget). Intended for infra planning and to feed Dedicated capacity commit recommendations for H1 2025 APAC expansion.

High-level notes / assumptions:
- Workload: mixed chat + completion + embedding bursts; 65% short interactions (<=128 tokens), 25% mid (129-512), 10% long (>512).
- Traffic profile (simulated): morning peak 07:00–10:00 JST, secondary peak 19:00–22:00 JST. Peak CBPM (concurrent backlogged model requests) used in sim: 18k at peak.
- GPU pool mix (target APAC region): H100-80GB x40, A100-80GB x60, L4 x200 (inference-optimized small instances).
- Models considered: rw-large v2 (32B), rw-medium v1 (7B), open-compat small (2.7B). Basel...

Scope and non-goals

text:
Purpose
This note defines recommended SDK surface, runtime flags, and integration patterns for making upstream Redwood clients region-aware. Goal: enable application and platform teams to respect customer region pins and enforce fallback behavior with minimal friction.

Scope and non-goals
- Scope: SDK behavior for server-side and backend client libraries (Node, Python, Java). Focus on client-side routing hints, local validation, telemetry, and test hooks.
- Non-goal: changing server routing or enforcement logic (that lives in control plane). This doc defines SDK-side controls and integration checklists only.

Background / problem statement
Customers can pin tenant traffic to a geographic region (e.g., eu-west-1) for compliance. If the SDK always routes to the default hosted endpoint, we lose early enforcement guarantees and observability. SDKs should:
 - surface customer pin expectat...

text:
Purpose:
This workbook is a compact, opinionated intake->vet->regress->rollout checklist for new model candidates. It is intentionally workflow-oriented: quick acceptance gates, short probe templates, and a lightweight regression cadence so product teams can decide fast and traceably.

How to use this doc:
- Fill the intake section and assign an owner before any compute is requested.
- Run the smoke probes locally or on dedicated dev capacity.
- If smoke passes, run the regression harness (see linked_artifacts).
- Use the decision matrix at the end to pick go/no-go and rollout plan.

1) Intake summary (one-paragraph):
- Model name / family: [e.g., mix-13b-quant]
- Provider / repo: [url or sha]
- Intended workload / product: [chat assistants, summarization, embeddings]
- Team owner: [name + email]
- Data sensitivity: public | internal | regulated

preview:

text:
Purpose:
This is a working sketch comparing options for multi-region control-plane topology for Redwood’s inference control plane. Focus: active-active vs hybrid active-passive, practical leader election approaches, split-brain avoidance, routing policy implications, and an operational DR/runbook sketch.

Context / constraints we care about:
- Global routing must preserve control-plane correctness for model rollouts (no split decisions that can cause double-rollouts).
- Latency SLO: control-plane interactions used by inference (model selection, routing hints) should not add >20ms median for hosted API decisions.
- Throughput: control-plane should sustain metadata updates (rollouts, config changes) at ~100 ops/sec during bursts without global contention.
- Failure modes: region partition, zone outage, control-plane process crash, DB region lag.
- Data residency: some customers require ...

text:
Purpose:
This doc captures an experimental set of patterns for tenant-scoped outbound (egress) by way of service proxy or gateway in customer VPC and on-prem installs. The aim is to settle on a small set of Terraform module primitives and an upgrade path that: 1) provides secure, auditable egress; 2) works for both cloud-VPC and air-gapped on-prem; 3) is compatible with Dedicated and Private deployment variants.

Context and constraints (quick):
- Customers expect predictable network egress controls (TLS inspection exceptions, proxy allow-lists, corporate outbound proxies).
- Private deployments range from full VPC in customer cloud, to colo racks with BGP, to fully air-gapped data centers with restricted egress (jump/egress appliances).
- We must support zero-downtime upgrades for runtime pods and minimal blast radius during TF module upgrades.
- Target latency budget for egress path...

Purpose:
This playbook (LiveLoop) is a compact, reproducible run-through to take a small app from first SDK call to an instrumented 10‑request demo in ~60 minutes. It prioritizes telemetry, shallow fallbacks, and minimal cost while producing a demo that highlights latency, token usage, and a simple quality signal.

Scope and audience:
- Audience: platform engineers, SDK maintainers, solutions engineers who need a repeatable demo scaffold and instrumentation baseline.
- Not intended: full production rollout checklists, enterprise contract items, deep security packaging.

High-level goals (measurable):
1) 1 hour to a working demo that: makes 10 generation requests, streams responses, records token/billing counters, and surfaces one simple QA metric (e.g., answer-length vs expected).
2) Telemetry events emitted for request.start, request.end, token.count, and fallback.used.
3) Automated ...

text:
Title: Data Residency Exception Memo (NordBank AB)

Purpose
This memo documents Redwood Inferences approval of a time-bounded exception to NordBank ABs requested EU-only data residency requirement to support conditional failover serving.

Baseline (normal operations)
- Primary serving region: EU (eu-west).
- Redwood does not enable prompt/response payload logging for NordBanks tenant.

Approved exception
- Exception: Conditional failover serving in US (us-east)
- Conditions: Only when EU region is degraded or during a scheduled disaster recovery (DR) test window.
- Duration: Through 2025-05-31.

Controls and scope
- Scope is *serving* only. Redwood will not intentionally persist prompts/responses as logs for NordBanks tenant.
- Operational metadata may be processed in the failover region during the approved windows (e.g., request ids, timings, aggregate error metrics).

Approvals
- Se...

title: Active-passive region routing simulation and runbook
preview:

This is a working note + operational runbook for active/passive regional steering (primary region + hot-standby) used during capacity failures, cross-region latency spikes, and control-plane instability. The doc mixes architecture rationale, quick-play mitigations, and simulation results. Treat as a living ...
text:
Purpose:
This is a working note + operational runbook for active/passive regional steering (primary region + hot-standby) used during capacity failures, cross-region latency spikes, and control-plane instability. The doc mixes architecture rationale, quick-play mitigations, and simulation results. Treat as a living artifact: iterate after each incident.

High-level goals:
- Keep <100ms added tail latency for 95th pct of requests when failing over.
- Avoid double-executing long-running requests during rollovers.
- Preserve model-version affinity where possible to avoid quality regressions.
- Provide deterministic, auditable decision steps for on-call.

Assumptions / constraints:
- Each customer has a primary region (P) and a configured passive region (S). Traffic normally routes to P.
- Control plane stores steering policies in CP config service with strong consistency (leader-based ra...

text:
Purpose:
This runbook captures pragmatic steps, validation checks, and Terraform (TF) workarounds we found while moving customer on‑prem stacks from staging to production in constrained environments. It is intended as a short, field‑friendly reference for SREs and infra engineers doing the cutover (not a policy doc).

Scope and assumptions:
- Applies to private deployments where control plane is hosted but inference nodes live in customer VPCs / on‑prem racks.
- Assumes: TF state is remote (consul/s3-like), state locking available, network peering or crossconnect already tested in staging.
- Does NOT cover application schema migrations or model weights transfer (separate playbook).

Quick summary of the approach:
1) Prepare: freeze infra changes, snapshot TF state, preflight tests in staging mirror.
2) Dry‑run: create a production namespace with traffic shadowing and smoke tests for 2...

text:
Purpose:
This working draft maps raw infra metrics (latency percentiles, uptime, token cost, cache hit rate) to customer-facing KPI definitions and a renewal signal rubric. Goal is a single compact table teams can reference during QBR prep, renewal risk scoring, and escalation playbooks.

Audience:
- CSMs preparing QBRs
- Solutions and SREs who supply metric extracts
- Rev/AMs who want a quantified renewal signal tied to operational observations

High-level approach (notes to self):
- Prefer percentile + SLO gap over simple averages (P95/P99 tell the story for tail latency)
- Use rolling 30d windows for renewal signals; use 7d windows for incident snapshots in review decks
- Convert metric deltas into a 0-100 renewal-risk score (see translation matrix)
- Maintain a minimal set of ownerable thresholds so CS team can act without engineering rework

KPI definitions (canonical) and calcul...

This doc captures an operationally-oriented protocol and checklist for handing requests across regions when capacity is constrained, and for progressively degrading model fidelity with minimal user impact. It is intended for platform engineers implementing control-plane routing logic and SREs v...
text:
Purpose and context:

This doc captures an operationally-oriented protocol and checklist for handing requests across regions when capacity is constrained, and for progressively degrading model fidelity with minimal user impact. It is intended for platform engineers implementing control-plane routing logic and SREs validating runbooks during incident drills.

High-level goals:
- Keep surface-level API availability at or above the SLA (99.9% availability for hosted tier) by shifting requests across regions and model variants when local capacity or latency envelopes are compromised.
- Minimize quality regressions by preferring smaller, controlled degradations (e.g., shorter context, quantized variant, lower compute family) over wholesale failures.
- Make routing decisions observable, reversible, and auditable via control-plane traces and rollout metadata.

Core concepts (compact):
- Fast...

title: Slice profiling: coldstart vs warm-start pairwise labels and NDCG workbook
preview:
Purpose and scope

This is a working workbook for profiling reranker performance across query slices that highlight differences between coldstart and warm-start behavior. Primary goal: produce a reproducible pairwise-label + NDCG evaluation flow that surfaces regressions specific to cold/warm populations and gives r...
text:
Purpose and scope

This is a working workbook for profiling reranker performance across query slices that highlight differences between coldstart and warm-start behavior. Primary goal: produce a reproducible pairwise-label + NDCG evaluation flow that surfaces regressions specific to cold/warm populations and gives reliable CIs for small slices. This doc is intentionally pragmatic — experiment notes, ad-hoc QC checks, and TODOs are included.

Background/context

Why coldstart vs warm-start? In production we observe two qualitative search/session patterns:
- coldstart: user issues first queries in a session (no cached KV/prefetch, query distribution more exploratory)
- warm-start: user issues follow-up queries or cursor/paginated interactions (benefits from cached context, user intent narrower)

Model variants may trade-off aggressive cross-document features (improve warm-start relevanc...

title: Observability-driven state repair and idempotent routing notes
preview:

Target audience: platform engineers working on routing, control-plane state, and multi-regio...
text:
Purpose and scope:

This doc collects experimental notes and decision heuristics for an approach we can trial that puts observability at the center of eventual consistency handling across the control plane and routing layer. Target audience: platform engineers working on routing, control-plane state, and multi-region resiliency.

Problem statement (short):
- We run a geo-distributed control plane with partial replicas (etcd + CRDB hybrid ideas) and local caches in front of regionally-deployed routers.
- Staleness and transient divergence are inevitable; we need a pragmatic, operationally-safe way to detect, repair, and gracefully degrade without inventing more synchronous cross-region coordination.

High-level idea: observability-driven state repair + idempotent routing.
- Use rich telemetry (per-request traces, divergence counters, cache-miss/validation events) to detect probable inc...
